title: Microgramma percussa
intro: Microgramma percussa, commonly known as the 'Rattle Fern', is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. Bioactive constituents of Microgramma percussa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Microgramma percussa include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms associated with colds, flu, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Microgramma percussa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of fern and a member of the genus Microgramma. This genus is a group of epiphytic ferns native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Microgramma percussa is often found growing on trees or rocks in shaded areas, where it can thrive in the humid climate. The plant's classification helps scientists understand its evolutionary history and its relationships with other plants within the Polypodiaceae family.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate various health conditions. Some studies suggest that extracts from Microgramma percussa may exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a potential natural remedy for infections. side_effects: Microgramma percussa, also known as the Pilea, is a plant commonly used in traditional medicine, but it may cause some side effects due to its toxicity.
